blocks.content: The Land of Fallen Giants, Muspelheim.
In a place where unquenchable flames stretched to the horizon, the fire giants lived.
Among the giants born from the elixir of myths, those who harbored the incarnation of hatred had nothing in their minds but burning hatred, with no clear purpose or cause.
Kariel walked leisurely through the battlefield of brawling giants, who were screaming and injuring each other.
They, too, had once been bound under Heaven's command structure, and they knew that breathing fire at the beautiful archangel would result in instant annihilation.
Surtr, the leader of the fire giants, came to greet Kariel.
Standing at 7 meters tall and clad in armor of flames, his presence was comparable to a deity, but even he knelt in the most reverent posture before the archangel.

blocks.speaker: Surtr
blocks.content: A humble being greets Lord Kariel.
The intense heat enveloped Kariel.
In Heaven, the biologically strongest being was undoubtedly Ymir, but the fire giants were somewhat unique compared to other giants.
They wielded their own unique magic.
This was likely due to the unstructured freedom of thought inherent in fire.
While Ymir's warrior could be compared to Ikael's three-horned Mara, Ashur, the strongest swordsman in purgatory was undoubtedly Surt.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: I have a task for you.
Surt looked up in surprise.
The fire sword Muspel, which he carried, spewed massive flames and dragged along the ground.

blocks.speaker: Surt
blocks.content: Entrust me with anything!
Kariel nodded in satisfaction.
Anke Ra had entrusted all authority to Ikael, and she would end the war.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: If that happened, Kariel's purpose would be thwarted, and with his limbs cut off, he had no way to oppose Ikael.
But they would come.
Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: He would surely come, as long as Miro was alive.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Though I hate to admit it…

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Miro was exceptional.
If humans had the most outstanding magician, the opportunity to change Heaven's situation could come at any time.
Now was not the time to be picky about gathering forces.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Prepare the elite troops and come to the Second Heaven, Lakia. I will be waiting in the Hall of Corruption.

blocks.speaker: Surt
blocks.content: It is the most fitting place for us.
The fire giants were banned from entering Heaven, but according to the principles of the law, the city of fallen angels, Lakia, was an exception, which was why Kariel had fled to the Second Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: There is no time. Depart immediately.
Having given his orders, Kariel flew straight to Heaven.
It was time to welcome the guests.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You have nothing to tell me?
Shirone couldn't believe Ikael's words.
No, perhaps it was true.
A vain delusion, or a selfish and utterly human expectation that it would be so.
Such thoughts might have caused the misunderstanding.
But how could he explain the faint memory lingering in his mind, and the feeling of his heart racing whenever he tried to touch that memory?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps…
Shirone couldn't bring himself to speak.
He couldn't bear to hear the same answer again, so he asked indirectly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who is Goffin?
Thud.
Ikael's halo trembled again.
If the angels had seen this, they would have been shocked to the point of losing their minds.
Ikael, realizing this, composed herself.
As Heaven's commander, she couldn't show any disarray while negotiating a truce with the rebels.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Goffin is the last of the Gaia. After losing the war with Heaven, he left the photon realm. Of course, all information is now lost.
Shirone knew about Goffin's erasure.
But how did Ikael have memories of him?
There was only one possible reason.
Just as humans inferred Goffin's existence through his relics, she must have personal memories of Goffin that had become objectified.
For humans who lived only about 100 years, this was impossible, but this was Heaven, the place most deeply connected to Goffin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Were you close?
Ikael shook her head.
Thinking about that time made her head ache.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: No. He was a stubborn human. He didn't know how to compromise. If it weren't for him, the Gaia wouldn't have suffered so many sacrifices.
Her reaction was subtly different from when Shirone first met Ikael in Heaven.
It was only natural.
According to Babel's records, Gaia and Heaven fought fiercely for each other's annihilation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So you met as enemies.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: We clashed countless times and argued endlessly. He was strong.
Even as an archangel, I couldn't settle things with him.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Once, there was this incident…
Ikael suddenly stopped speaking.
She couldn't remember anything.
It felt like a black hole had punched through the space filled with colorful memories.
As she stared into that black space, all her emotions were sucked in, and a crushing pain filled her chest.
Ikael widened her eyes and bit her lip.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Not now. I can't be underestimated any longer.
She amplified her voice and addressed everyone.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I will change the negotiator. The person in charge, come here.
Shirone became desperate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why are you deceiving me?

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: About what? I'm not deceiving anything.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You're different from before! There must be a reason you taught me Ataraxia!

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: It was to keep Kariel in check. Thanks to that, I was able to return to the position of archangel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, you have to tell me. You have that responsibility.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: My only responsibility is to Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You are…!
Shirone stood up abruptly.
Both of them were avoiding the core issue, arguing with just their energy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You are the one who…!
Just as Shirone was about to shout, the door burst open.
Commander Crude, Gaold's party, and the officers, having heard Ikael's message, rushed in.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What's going on?
As soon as Plu entered the room, she checked Shirone's condition.
Fortunately, the feared incident hadn't occurred, but the atmosphere was tense.
Ikael was glaring at Shirone angrily, and Shirone was clenching his fists and biting his lip.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I admit my mistake. I don't want to drag this out any longer. State your demands. Heaven's only condition is the cessation of war.
Ikael had already reverted to the dignified archangel. And to Shirone, that was all she was.
Everyone moved to the command control room and sat around the round table.
Some people had no seats and stood, including Shirone and Plu.
Plu found it strange that Shirone, who had insisted on a meeting with Ikael, was now staring at the wall, ignoring her.
It was puzzling why Shirone had set the condition of meeting Ikael, and she wondered if there could be anything in the world that would make them both blush while sitting alone with an archangel.

blocks.speaker: Commander Crude
blocks.content: These are our conditions. Return the rebels to Heaven. Ban the elixir of life. Also, strip the Kergoin of their sacrificial rights and elect leaders from all races. These three things.
Ikael's eyes calmed down.
Why did humans love equality so much?
Nothing in this world is truly equal. Yet they hold equality as their highest value.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Because they are weak.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Yes, they are weak.
Therefore, this is not subjugation but mercy for the weak.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I accept. You will no longer suffer from the elixir of life.

blocks.speaker: Debra
blocks.content: That's not enough.
An officer who had been standing against the wall stepped forward.
He had been glaring at Ikael with fierce eyes since he arrived, having lost his family in the war with Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Debra
blocks.content: Do you know how much we suffered because of you? And now you want to end it as if nothing happened?

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Debra, restrain yourself.
Crude tried to stop him, but Debra couldn't hold back his anger.

blocks.speaker: Debra
blocks.content: Shouldn't you suffer the same pain? One of your angels should apologize and commit suicide! That's my condition!
Equality.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Understood. I will comply.
The round table murmured.
Debra might have lost his reason, but no one expected him to allow an angel's suicide.
Then another officer, who had been watching, stepped forward.

blocks.speaker: Officer
blocks.content: Give us immortality.
He was a man with a malignant tumor spreading through his body, with little time left to live.

blocks.speaker: Officer
blocks.content: You don't die even if you're killed, right? So what's the big deal about one angel dying? Give us immortality! That's the condition!
Equality.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: How far will you indulge, humans?
Chiiiiing!
Ikael's halo expanded, and Ataraxia was instantly concentrated.
As Gaold pressed down with an air press, the round table flattened.
Ikael, who had already left her seat, flew through the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Damn it! Chase her!
Ikael's trademark Ataraxia amplifies all kinds of power in the world.
That's why it's the strongest.
Even a gentle spring breeze could turn into a typhoon capable of sweeping the world if Ataraxia was applied.
Sein activated Equilibrium with his iron wheel eye, but it wasn't enough to reverse Ikael's information.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Attack! If we don't capture her now, it's the end!
Crude glared at the officers who had ruined the negotiations and then rushed outside.
He couldn't blame them. Even he had momentarily hoped for something.
Ikael was amplifying the underground water veins flowing beneath the command center.
The amplification was at least 20,000 times.
The moment the water veins burst through, everything in the command center would be destroyed.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: As the archangel, I command you.
Ataraxia shot a brilliant light into the sky.
Annihilate.
The ground shook vertically, and cracks appeared on the floor.
As the iron buildings began to tear like paper, despair spread across the humans' faces.
Gaold's assessment was accurate.
Ikael was not something to be defined by the concept of combat; she was sheer power itself.
Target for elimination: Archangel Ikael.
Just as a massive explosion was about to occur, a black object flew rapidly towards Ikael.
To everyone else, it was a blur, but to Ikael, it was clearly Babel, as if it were etched in her memory.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: What is this? What did I forget that my mind is…
After what felt like over ten thousand thoughts by human standards, Babel reached Ikael.
She twisted her body leisurely.
At that moment, an unknown force restrained her.
As she quickly turned her gaze, she saw Shirone, transformed into Vajra Armor, activating Akamai's Antithesis.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Shirone…?
Bang!
Babel's fist struck Ikael's face.
Exposed to the tremendous force, Ikael's body plummeted vertically, but just before hitting the ground, she spread her golden wings to stop the fall.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: This… this can't be…
The rebels, ready for battle, stood with their mouths agape.
Even Gaold was taken aback this time.
As Ikael gracefully rose from her prone position, Shirone landed on the ground.
The tendrils of the Vajra Armor spread like a peacock's feathers, aiming at Ikael, while Akamai's eyes on the tendrils locked onto her.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Antithesis, huh.
Ikael shrugged off the restraining force with a shake of her shoulders.
It could certainly bind a lower angel.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: He had become much stronger than before.
But what use was it? There was nothing in the world stronger than her.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Shirone, if you intend to oppose me like this…
Thud!
The sound of the ground shaking stopped Ikael in her tracks.
The metal angel Babel stood beside Shirone, ready for battle.
Ikael frowned slightly.
The fact that a being resembling her was protecting Shirone was extremely displeasing.
